statement.en: Decision responsiveness and the legitimacy of public agencies data set
statement.summary: The data were collected in relation to the following goals: The research was designed to identify some of the key factors driving assessments of the legitimacy of decision-making agencies. The research was based on a set of (vignette-based) experiments that allowed the researchers to identify the effects on legitimacy of various aspects of decision-making responsiveness. Participants (N=438) were drawn from Prolific Academic, and completed an online survey fielded on Qualtrics. Assignment to treatment groups was randomised.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint dataset (2026-04-25 timestamp, appears prospective or recent) describing empirical research on legitimacy perception. The core claims are provisional: (1) causal effects of responsiveness on legitimacy assessments depend on validation of experimental design, statistical analysis, and replication; (2) vignette-based experiments generate ecologically bounded findings; (3) the dataset itself is a research artifact, not a proposition amenable to true/false classification. Status is 'under change' pending peer review, replication studies, and integration with legitimacy theory.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 応答性の時間積分が正当性の深さを決める、という直感。
